questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the '3 By 35 Initiative' recently seen in the news:
1. It is an initiative launched by the World Health Organization (WHO).
2. It aims to increase the prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 35% by the year 2030.
3. It promotes the use of health taxes to address the rising burden of non-communicable diseases (NCDs).

How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The 3 By 35 Initiative was launched by the World Health Organization (WHO). Statement 2 is incorrect: The initiative aims to raise the prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% (not 35%) by the year 2035 (not 2030). Statement 3 is correct: It recommends health taxes as a cost-effective tool to address population levels of obesity and other related non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
questions.takeaway: The WHO's 3 By 35 Initiative targets a 50% price increase on three unhealthy products (tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks) by 2035 using health taxes to combat NCDs.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: WHO launched 3 By 35 Initiatives, aims to raise prices of any or all of three unhealthy products tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035... WHO recommends taxation as one of the most cost-effective tools for addressing population levels of obesity and other related non-communicable diseases (NCDs).

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt:
With reference to 'Health Taxes' as recommended by the World Health Organization (WHO), consider the following statements:
1. They are levied primarily on products that have a negative public health impact to address non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
2. They are considered one of the most cost-effective tools for addressing population levels of obesity.
3. They are exclusively applied to ultra-processed packaged foods and trans-fats.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Health taxes are levied on products with a negative public health impact (e.g., tobacco) to address NCDs. Statement 2 is correct: The WHO recommends taxation as one of the most cost-effective tools for addressing population levels of obesity. Statement 3 is incorrect: Health taxes are not exclusively applied to ultra-processed foods; the WHO specifically targets products like tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks under its initiatives.
questions.takeaway: Health taxes are a cost-effective WHO-recommended tool levied on unhealthy products like tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks to combat obesity and non-communicable diseases.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: What is Health Tax? Levied on products that have a negative public health impact e.g. Tobacco, etc. WHO recommends taxation as one of the most cost-effective tools for addressing population levels of obesity and other related non-communicable diseases (NCDs).

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the primary objective of the '3 By 35 Initiative' recently seen in the news?

options.text: To eliminate three major communicable diseases—tuberculosis, malaria, and HIV/AIDS—in 35 high-burden countries.
To ensure that 35% of the global population has access to three essential primary healthcare services by 2035.
To raise the prices of tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035.
To reduce out-of-pocket expenditure on healthcare to below 35% of total health spending in developing nations.

questions.explanation: The 3 By 35 Initiative, launched by the WHO, aims to raise the prices of any or all of three unhealthy products—tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks—by at least 50% through health taxes by the year 2035.
questions.takeaway: The '3' in the 3 By 35 Initiative refers to three products (tobacco, alcohol, sugary drinks), and '35' refers to the target year 2035.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: WHO launched 3 By 35 Initiatives, aims to raise prices of any or all of three unhealthy products tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035.

questions.subject: Science
questions.prompt: Which of the following combinations of products is specifically targeted for price increases under the World Health Organization's '3 By 35 Initiative'?

options.text: Alcohol, red meat, and sodium-rich foods
Sugary drinks, artificial sweeteners, and tobacco

questions.explanation: The WHO's '3 By 35 Initiative' explicitly aims to raise the prices of any or all of three specific unhealthy products: tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks, by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035.
questions.takeaway: The '3' in the 3 By 35 Initiative refers to the three targeted products: tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: WHO launched 3 By 35 Initiatives, aims to raise prices of any or all of three unhealthy products tobacco, alcohol, and sugary drinks by at least 50% through health taxes by 2035.
